---
title: "Data Visualization Final Project - Chicago Violence, SVI and Social Metrics"
author: "Salvatore 'Sal' Macchione and Harry Franklin"
date: "March 09, 2026"
format: 
  pdf:
    include-in-header: 
       text: |
         \usepackage{fvextra}
         \DefineVerbatimEnvironment{Highlighting}{Verbatim}{breaklines,commandchars=\\\{\}}
include-before-body:
  text: |
    \RecustomVerbatimEnvironment{verbatim}{Verbatim}{
      showspaces = false,
      showtabs = false,
      breaksymbolleft={},
      breaklines
    }
output:
  echo: false
  eval: false
---
# Introduction, Background and Research Question

From the notorious mob violence of the 1920s to the predominantly gang-related crime of today, Chicago has long been grappling with violent crime. Though crime has overall declined over the last several years, the city still makes regional and national headlines — justifiably or not — for its relatively high rates of crime. Though many analyses and dissections of crime in Chicago have been done, there exists a gap in existing data as it pertains to two key questions:
